title: Stake USDT - Open Aave App
meta_description: stake usdt on Aave by supplying USDT to a liquidity market; review live rates, network, and risks before opening the app.
hero_h2: Put stablecoin liquidity to work in a live market
hero_lead: stake usdt routes a stablecoin balance into an Aave supply market where interest is variable, on-chain, and dependent on the selected network and reserve.
what_is: Aave is a decentralized non-custodial liquidity protocol where users can supply assets to markets and earn variable interest; for USDT, this is lending-market participation, not native proof-of-stake. Read the Aave documentation before connecting a wallet.

how.d: Go to app.aave.com and connect a supported wallet on the network that holds your USDT.
Select the USDT market, confirm the reserve is active, and review the live supply APY, liquidity, and risk details in the interface.
Approve the ERC-20 spend or use permit if available, then confirm the supply transaction from your wallet.
Monitor the position in Aave and withdraw when desired, subject to available liquidity and network fees.

points.p: USDT does not have native validator staking; Aave supply returns come from borrower demand and protocol market parameters.
Aave markets use smart contracts, so wallet approvals, chain selection, and transaction review matter before funds move.
Check issuer and network information such as Tether transparency before transferring stablecoins.

faq.a: No. USDT is a stablecoin, not a proof-of-stake asset; on Aave, users supply USDT to a liquidity market and may earn variable interest.

faq.q: How do I stake usdt through Aave?
faq.a: Open the official app, connect a wallet, choose the USDT reserve, review live terms, approve the token, and confirm the supply transaction.
No. Aave supply APY is variable and can change with utilization, market conditions, and governance-approved parameters shown in the app.

faq.q: What risks matter before I stake usdt?
faq.a: Consider smart contract risk, stablecoin issuer risk, liquidity availability, network fees, wallet approvals, and regional access limits.

faq.q: Which network should I use to stake usdt?
faq.a: Use the network where your USDT is available and where Aave lists an active reserve; verify the selected chain inside the app before signing.

footer_note: Not the official Aave app; verify app.aave.com before connecting a wallet. Educational only, not financial advice.
